answer:
\boxed{A}  

The study shows that hydrophilic guidewires improved technical success rates for iliac occlusions (from 60% to 96%), femoral stenosis (84% to 100%), and femoral occlusions (64% to 96% for lesions <10 cm, 48% to 88% for lesions >10 cm), with significant differences noted. Only iliac stenosis showed no significant improvement. Therefore, overall, hydrophilic guidewires significantly improve success rates for most peripheral PTA cases.
